compliance_cache_used: sprint/compliance-cache/georgia.json (reused minimum_wage, overtime, federal-floor structure from existing GA cache; layered federal FLSA detail for exempt salary floor, tip credit, PUMP Act, meal-break rules drawn from US DOL + 29 USC/29 CFR primary sources)
high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty: federal exempt salary floor cited at $35,568 / $684 per week. This reflects the 2019 rule (29 CFR Part 541) restored after Texas federal court (Northern District) vacated the 2024 DOL rule that lifted the threshold to $43,888. Court ruling: November 2024 (Plano Chamber of Commerce v. DOL). Verify the 2026 rule is still operative against US DOL WHD updates before any client-pricing decision; further DOL rulemaking is possible under the new administration.

factual_uncertainty: PUMP Act 2022 (29 U.S.C. § 207(r)) — confirmed coverage of FLSA non-exempt employees; reasonable break time + private space (not a toilet); one year after birth. Verify with DOL PUMP Act resource page for any 2025-2026 amendments.

factual_uncertainty: Georgia child-labour break rule for minors under 16 working 5+ hours (30-min break). Statute cited as OCGA § 39-2-2; older state code section — verify current renumbering under Georgia Department of Labor child labour rules before relying.

prompt_correction: prompt stated 'NO state mandate' for meal/rest breaks for adults. Verified accurate. Carved out the under-16 minor exception (OCGA-mandated 30-min break after 5 consecutive hours) on the page to avoid misleading employers with mixed-age workforces.
link_gate_warn: Georgia parent + Alabama/Florida sibling wage pages rendered as plain text per link gate (targets ship wave 3-4)
secondary_cta_rule_check: secondary CTA in masthead uses canonical Employer Cost Calculator URL; tagline-close secondary uses Crossover Calculator. Both real destinations on www.teamed.global.
